close_reason: Positive observation: in-thread signing is correct for lightweight ops
description:
lib.rs:178-185 signs Ed25519 and P-256 directly on the tokio executor thread; only RSA-PSS uses spawn_blocking.

This is correct. Ed25519 sign is ~10 µs and P-256 ECDSA sign with rfc6979 is ~50-100 µs on commodity x86_64. RSA-2048 PSS sign is ~1-3 ms which IS a meaningful executor-blocking quantum. The threshold for 'must use spawn_blocking' on the cooperative tokio scheduler is conventionally ~100 µs to 1 ms; both fast curves are below it.

A reviewer applying a blanket 'always spawn_blocking for crypto' rule would be wrong: the spawn cost itself is ~10-20 µs, so for Ed25519 it would more than double signing latency for no benefit.

The crate-level comment at lib.rs:187-191 already explains this. DEFEND.
title: DEFEND: Ed25519/P-256 sign in-thread without spawn_blocking
